description_long: This is a medieval-style battle axe featuring a broad, wedge-shaped steel blade on one side and a pointed spike on the opposite side. The blade is robust and slightly curved, with a sharp edge for chopping and a flat poll for balance. The spike is long and tapered, designed for piercing. The handle is cylindrical, made of dark-stained wood, and reinforced with metal rivets along the upper section for added durability. The overall construction suggests a tool built for both cutting and puncturing, with a utilitarian yet formidable appearance. The materials and finish give it a rugged, functional look suitable for heavy-duty use.
description: A medieval-style battle axe with a wide steel blade, pointed spike, and a reinforced wooden handle.
description_view_0: The broad steel blade and pointed spike are clearly visible, with the wooden handle and metal rivets facing forward.
description_view_1: The axe is seen edge-on, showing the thin profile of the blade and spike, with the handle appearing as a narrow cylinder.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the blade and spike are visible, mirroring view 0 but from the other side.
description_view_3: The axe is viewed directly from the top or bottom, showing the pointed spike and the cylindrical handle in alignment.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, dark-stained, with visible metal rivets for reinforcement, no curvature or special grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Wide wedge-shaped steel blade with a single sharp edge and a long, pointed spike on the opposite side; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional mounting with visible rivets or pins securing the blade to the wooden handle.
